title: Click ID catalogue — 5 supported ad-network click IDs
description: Canonical mapping of ad-network click IDs to their extraction rules + provider metadata. Read by ClickIdExtractor at request boundary + AttributionSnapshotBuilder for the provider_hint. Extending: add a new provider entry here + no schema migration required (click IDs live in the jsonb blob).

providers.pattern_description: Alphanumeric + dashes + underscores. Length typically 40-100 chars but Google occasionally emits longer.
providers.notes: The single largest source of paid click attribution. Almost every Wave 5 marketing-forwarder will support Google.

providers.pattern_description: Alphanumeric + dashes + underscores. Length typically 30-80 chars.

_fbc.description: Meta-formatted click ID (fb.subdomain-index.timestamp.fbclid). Auto-computed by ClickIdExtractor from fbclid + captured_at when _fbc cookie is absent.

_fbp.description: Meta browser ID cookie (fb.subdomain-index.timestamp.random). Persists across sessions; captured as supplementary attribution signal.

providers.notes: Meta CAPI expects _fbc (formatted click ID) rather than raw fbclid. ClickIdExtractor computes _fbc from fbclid when the browser didn't set it (older SDKs).

providers.pattern_description: Alphanumeric + dashes + underscores. Length typically 40-100 chars.
providers.notes: Smaller cookie window (30 days) reflects TikTok's shorter conversion window.

providers.pattern_description: Lowercase hex — 32 chars is the typical shape. Microsoft is stricter about format than Google.
providers.notes: Bing captures msclkid distinct from gclid — a user searching on Bing sees msclkid; on Google sees gclid. Both may be present on multi-search sessions.

providers.pattern_description: Alphanumeric + dashes + underscores. Length typically 40-100 chars.
providers.notes: Note the query param uses PascalCase 'ScCid' while the cookie uses lowercase '_sccid' — Snapchat's convention.

provider_hint_precedence.description: When multiple click IDs are present on the same request (multi-touch attribution across ad networks), the provider_hint field on the click_id jsonb captures the highest-precedence provider. Rationale: Google typically dominates paid budget; Meta second; TikTok third; Microsoft fourth; Snapchat fifth. Tenants may override via config attribution.click_ids.provider_hint_precedence.

order: 2. Fall back to cookies (persisted click from earlier session).
3. Persist any query-param-sourced click IDs to cookies for future requests.

cookie_options.notes: http_only=false so client-side JS analytics libraries can also read the cookies (dual-source pattern). same_site=Lax to survive cross-site redirects from ad clicks.

consent_gate.description: Extraction is gated by advertising consent — not marketing consent. A subject may grant marketing (basic UTM capture) but withhold advertising (click ID capture). When advertising consent is off: click_id is stored as { provider_hint: null } — the utm_* fields survive.

notes: Every provider added here must land with (a) pattern regex, (b) CAPI endpoint (for downstream marketing module Wave 5), (c) supplementary_cookies if any, (d) gate.
The gate field determines which tier receives capture. Small tier gets google + meta only; Medium+ unlocks tiktok + microsoft + snapchat.
Every provider added here has a corresponding entry in the marketing::providers registry (Wave 5) for CAPI forwarding.
Adding a 6th provider (e.g. Pinterest — currently no dedicated click ID; uses utm_source only) requires ZERO schema migration. The click_id jsonb absorbs new keys naturally.
